source.title: Mechanisms of systems memory consolidation during sleep

confidence_rationale: Comprehensive field review synthesising the state of mechanistic evidence on mode-specific consolidation. Three explicit claims bear directly on MECH-261: (i) SWS and REM route information through distinct oscillation families with distinct downstream effects on synaptic plasticity; (ii) the oscillation geometry (not just mode identity) regulates information flow across distant brain networks; (iii) memory transformation during consolidation is qualitative (gist-like abstraction), not mere copy. Not primary data, but synthesises the mode-gating literature MECH-261 is drawing from and provides the canonical citation framework.
failure_signatures: A MECH-261 implementation that treats mode as an on/off scalar without distinguishing the carrier rhythms per mode will fail to capture the qualitative difference between SWS consolidation (abstraction and schema integration) and REM consolidation (prior-reset / precision recalibration).

A single mode gate per target that does not differentiate forward SWS-consolidation from REM-recalibration conflates two functionally dissociable outputs; ablating this distinction predicts that REE's sleep passes will either over-consolidate (SWS-only mode) or over-reset (REM-only mode) because the normal antagonism is collapsed.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Sleep-dependent systems consolidation is driven by mode-specific hippocampal replay gated by characteristic brain oscillations (slow oscillations + spindles + ripples in SWS; theta in REM) that regulate information flow across distant brain networks and local synaptic plasticity, producing qualitatively abstracted, gist-like memory representations.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-261's operating-mode write-gate registry is the compute-side realisation of the mode-specific routing this review characterises. The SWS gate routes hippocampus-to-mPFC schema integration; the REM gate does something functionally distinct (prior reset / theta-phase reverse replay per Boyce 2016, Poe 2017). In REE the SalienceCoordinator's mode probabilities select among these. The review is the authoritative synthesis on which papers are load-bearing evidence for which mode and why -- it is the citation scaffold for future MECH-261 evidence pulls.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Reviews weight less than primary data in governance but are indispensable for understanding the mapping. Klinzing, Niethard and Born are a primary source of the 'active systems consolidation' framing that REE's SD-017 sleep pass architecture draws from; MECH-261 is the gate-level primitive that active-systems-consolidation requires. Treat this entry as the scaffold for the rest of the pull and not as independent evidence.
mapping.source_context: Narrative review covering rodent and human literature on NREM/REM mechanisms, hippocampal replay, thalamocortical coupling, SHY, and qualitative transformation of memory during consolidation.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature Neuroscience review, authoritative authors, widely cited). Mapping fidelity high because the review explicitly characterises the per-mode gating architecture MECH-261 instantiates. Transfer risk low because the review synthesises both rodent and human data and flags the homology caveats. Confidence capped at 0.80 because it is a review rather than primary data -- the primary-data claims it synthesises are in the companion entries.
